content.full_text:
File -
84212 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Barbara Campbel~ 
Filing of Candidate's Short Form 
Date 
March 30, 1979 
In determining whether a candidate can file a short 
form campaign statement (Government Code Section 84212) 
the candidate must consider controlled committees and 
other committees that make independent expenditures in 
support of his or her candidacy in determining that not 
more than $200 has been spent. 
BC:plh

sections.conclusion: In determining whether a candidate can file a short form campaign statement (Government Code Section 84212) the candidate must consider controlled committees and other committees that make independent expenditures in support of his or her candidacy in determining that not more than $200 has been spent.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a candidate can file a short form campaign statement under Government Code Section 84212 and what committees must be considered when determining if spending thresholds are met.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Candidates filing a short form campaign statement must consider both controlled committees and committees making independent expenditures on their behalf when determining if spending remains under the $200 threshold.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers. Content structure suggests question/answer format but without formal section demarcation. Conclusion extracted from body text. Brief advisory letter format.
